item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, if you WANT it to be the same character (or leave it ambiguous in-universe), I'd be happy to link 4581 from the footnote here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is not! This is absolutely wild, but SJolene apparently beat me to this nickname by almost a whole year. I only stumbled on this skip today because of an unrelated search.

In short: upvoted for a fun concept, plus eerie synchronicity!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is MxMaster the same person from SCP-4581, Anti-Racist Audiocassette? And if so, does this qualify as a Gamers Against Weed entry?
Wow. Thanks, everyone - I really wasn't expecting this kind of reception for my first SCP!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4496 is anomalously durable compared to other "Randy" action figures, lacking the excess fragility that typically characterizes the toys

I slammed that upvote button the second I saw this. I love it and how it specifically gets around that. A +1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A Transformers SCP?

About a Beast Wars toy?

And GPS?!

+1. Nice little story, especially for collectors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not immediately, I don't think? It reduces intermolecular bonds to the point of making things around it brittle and fragile, not to the point of total structural collapse. It would make anyone affected WAY more susceptible to potentially fatal injury, but the two individuals actually affected here got lucky (and also got Foundation medical care immediately after the second incident).
But maybe that's complete nonsense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On conversion to its robot form, intermolecular forces within approximately 4.550 meters of SCP-4496 will be instantaneously and significantly weakened

Wouldn't this be like, super fucking fatal?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
as its ability to deteriorate matter around it is quite dangerous.

Object class is based on difficulty of containment, not potential for danger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I get what you mean, but ultimately, I thought the way I structured it made for a comedic beat to end on; plus, a couple of people who I previewed it to didn't initially get the idea that making everything around it fragile was tied to the toy's anomalous durability, so I wanted to make that explicit. If you have any ideas of how to get that across while not being so forthright, though, I'd love to hear them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think so. If you lock it in a box in robot mode it isn't going to trigger its anomalous effect, which, per the Locked Box Test, makes it Safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand where you're coming from - there's a certain mystique lost - but at the same time, I couldn't mentally justify the Foundation not finding this stuff out, and I thought the email logs a more entertaining and characterful way of explaining it than "they asked the owner and he explained what happened".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an excellent first SCP, however, I feel like you may want to change its class to Euclid, as its ability to deteriorate matter around it is quite dangerous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really good-quality first SCP. Interesting concept, and it's well-written too! The one gripe I have is that there's little left to wonder about it. Every important question seems to be answered here, with no real room for interpretations or theories. Regardless, it's a very clean and inventive piece for a first SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not wild about the e-mail logs. I still upvoted, but I think the piece would be stronger and have a little more sense of mystery without them stating directly what's going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice little SCP, interesting anomalous properties, reasonable recovery, and the emails were great. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first skip - a silly little tale about why toy collectors shouldn't solicit help from anartists. Thanks to users IAmTheOoga and mayoculpa for their help with it.
I'd appreciate any criticism the community can offer!
